metadata.title:
CITY BEAUTIFUL NOW


THE CITY b0UNT|FUL

pages.raw_text:
terday sat among the bundles of
clothing repairing and patching the
women's and children's goods.


The report yesterday that fire.
arms were needed in Fernie for
fear Of depredations by Slavonians,
was altogether uncalled for, as w.
d. Finley ascertained on his ar
rival within the fire zone. The
Slavs and in fact foreigners of all
descriptions worked shoulder to
shoulder with the Canadians and
Americans assisting the wounded
and taking care of exposed prop
erty.


All day long Mr., Dreher has been
In receipt of kilograms from the
striker people thanking the people
of Spokane for their prompt and
ready answer to the call for help.
Spokane was the only point from
which aid could be sent for the
first "a hours and the people here
responded with their customary
energy and sympathy.


Considerable credit is due to
Acting Mayor Joe Phillips, who
hustled about among the council
men and was authorized to go to
any needed expense. The first day
of disaster was Sunday and most
people were away. Phillips. how
ever, with Pres. Goodall Of the
chamber of commerce, got into
communication with merchants and
got carload Of goods of as soon
as possible. The councilmen will
make an appropriation to cover
what expense was undertaken. 1t
will not amount to more than 5000
or $500, which will be taken out
of the general fund.


Pres. Goodall of the chamber OF
commerce did transfers duty
much of yesterday, personally as
sisting in loading wagons and
handling the reins In driving the
goods down to the relief train at
the o. r. & N. depot.
